에스겔 18:1.content: Yahweh’s word came to me again, saying,

에스겔 18:2.content: “What do you mean, that you use this proverb concerning the land of Israel, saying, ‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children’s teeth are set on edge’?

에스겔 18:3.content: “As I live,” says the Lord Yahweh, “you shall not use this proverb any more in Israel.

에스겔 18:4.content: Behold, all souls are mine; as the soul of the father, so also the soul of the son is mine. The soul who sins, he shall die.

에스겔 18:5.content: “But if a man is just, and does that which is lawful and right,

에스겔 18:6.content: and has not eaten on the mountains, hasn’t lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, hasn’t defiled his neighbor’s wife, hasn’t come near a woman in her impurity,

에스겔 18:7.content: and has not wronged any, but has restored to the debtor his pledge, has taken nothing by robbery, has given his bread to the hungry, and has covered the naked with a garment;

에스겔 18:8.content: he who hasn’t lent to them with interest, hasn’t taken any increase from them, who has withdrawn his hand from iniquity, has executed true justice between man and man,

에스겔 18:9.content: has walked in my statutes, and has kept my ordinances, to deal truly; he is just, he shall surely live,” says the Lord Yahweh.

에스겔 18:10.content: “If he fathers a son who is a robber who sheds blood, and who does any one of these things,

에스겔 18:11.content: or who does not do any of those things, but even has eaten at the mountain shrines, and defiled his neighbor’s wife,

에스겔 18:12.content: has wronged the poor and needy, has taken by robbery, has not restored the pledge, and has lifted up his eyes to the idols, has committed abomination,

에스겔 18:13.content: has lent with interest, and has taken increase from the poor; shall he then live? He shall not live. He has done all these abominations. He shall surely die. His blood will be on him.

에스겔 18:14.content: “Now, behold, if he fathers a son, who sees all his father’s sins, which he has done, and fears, and does not such like;

에스겔 18:15.content: who hasn’t eaten on the mountains, hasn’t lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, hasn’t defiled his neighbor’s wife,

에스겔 18:16.content: hasn’t wronged any, hasn’t taken anything to pledge, hasn’t taken by robbery, but has given his bread to the hungry, and has covered the naked with a garment;

에스겔 18:17.content: who has withdrawn his hand from the poor, who hasn’t received interest or increase, has executed my ordinances, has walked in my statutes; he shall not die for the iniquity of his father. He shall surely live.

에스겔 18:18.content: As for his father, because he cruelly oppressed, robbed his brother, and did that which is not good among his people, behold, he will die in his iniquity.

에스겔 18:19.content: “Yet you say, ‘Why doesn’t the son bear the iniquity of the father?’ When the son has done that which is lawful and right, and has kept all my statutes, and has done them, he will surely live.

에스겔 18:20.content: The soul who sins, he shall die. The son shall not bear the iniquity of the father, neither shall the father bear the iniquity of the son. The righteousness of the righteous shall be on him, and the wickedness of the wicked shall be on him.

에스겔 18:21.content: “But if the wicked turns from all his sins that he has committed, and keeps all my statutes, and does that which is lawful and right, he shall surely live. He shall not die.

에스겔 18:22.content: None of his transgressions that he has committed will be remembered against him. In his righteousness that he has done, he shall live.

에스겔 18:23.content: Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked?” says the Lord Yahweh; “and not rather that he should return from his way, and live?

에스겔 18:24.content: “But when the righteous turns away from his righteousness, and commits iniquity, and does according to all the abominations that the wicked man does, should he live? None of his righteous deeds that he has done will be remembered. In his trespass that he has trespassed, and in his sin that he has sinned, in them he shall die.

에스겔 18:25.content: “Yet you say, ‘The way of the Lord is not equal.’ Hear now, house of Israel: Is my way not equal? Aren’t your ways unequal?

에스겔 18:26.content: When the righteous man turns away from his righteousness, and commits iniquity, and dies therein; in his iniquity that he has done he shall die.

에스겔 18:27.content: Again, when the wicked man turns away from his wickedness that he has committed, and does that which is lawful and right, he will save his soul alive.

에스겔 18:28.content: Because he considers, and turns away from all his transgressions that he has committed, he shall surely live. He shall not die.

에스겔 18:29.content: Yet the house of Israel says, ‘The way of the Lord is not fair.’ House of Israel, aren’t my ways fair? Aren’t your ways unfair?

에스겔 18:30.content: “Therefore I will judge you, house of Israel, everyone according to his ways,” says the Lord Yahweh. “Return, and turn yourselves from all your transgressions; so iniquity will not be your ruin.

에스겔 18:31.content: Cast away from you all your transgressions, in which you have transgressed; and make yourself a new heart and a new spirit: for why will you die, house of Israel?

에스겔 18:32.content: For I have no pleasure in the death of him who dies,” says the Lord Yahweh. “Therefore turn yourselves, and live!
